creator: Draper, Thomas R.
title: Babtist Church After Flood
description: Real photo view of a building in ruins. The card also reads, "Draper."
subject: North and Central America; United States; North Carolina; Postcards--North Carolina; Disaster; Churches; Baptist churches
transcriptionnote: Message on back of card reads: "Bless your heart. I wish I could do something to keep you. I tried to talk to the hospital yesterday but could not raise Norfolk. When are you coming home - John[illegible] T." Addressed to: "Mrs Eva Way c/o Miss Anna Rose Cohn, [Illegible] Norfolka Va."

title: Roanoke Mills Co., Flood March 18th 1912, Roanoke Rapids, N.C.
description: A photograph of a flood surrounding a mill building.
subject: North and Central America; United States; North Carolina; Halifax County; Roanoke Rapids; Postcards--North Carolina; Floods; Mills; Textile industry; Disasters; Roanoke Rapids (N.C.); Halifax County (N.C.)
transcriptionnote: Message on back of card: "Hello, Cold here to day looks like snow but hope not. Ask Harry if he knows a carpenter there by the name of Kerns. He is the carpenter foreman here now come in to day will write you soon. Am well but Sut is sick to night. Love to all, B.D.B." Addressed to "Mrs. J.E. Patrick, 1214 Hamilton Ave., Newport News, Virginia." Postmarked February [1913].
